## Overview

To stream media in your browser app, create an instance of `MediaEnvironment` in the app. In the app, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/activate()] before you begin any media playback or capture, including using the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AVFoundation/AVCaptureSession] that you get by calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/makeCaptureSession()]. When you are done with the media environment, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/suspend()].

If you capture media input or prepare streaming content in your browser’s rendering extension, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/activate()] before calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/RenderingProcess/grantCapability(_:)] to grant a media playback and capture capability, which you create with [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/ProcessCapability/mediaPlaybackAndCapture(environment:)]. Call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/createXPCRepresentation()] and use [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/XPC] to send the media environment to the rendering extension. Additionally, grant the same capability to the web content extension for the page that’s playing or capturing media, by calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/WebContentProcess/grantCapability(_:)].

# MediaEnvironment

**Structure**

An object that identifies a media playback or streaming environment.

## Declaration

```swift
struct MediaEnvironment
```

## Overview

To stream media in your browser app, create an instance of `MediaEnvironment` in the app. In the app, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/activate()] before you begin any media playback or capture, including using the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/AVFoundation/AVCaptureSession] that you get by calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/makeCaptureSession()]. When you are done with the media environment, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/suspend()].

If you capture media input or prepare streaming content in your browser’s rendering extension, call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/activate()] before calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/RenderingProcess/grantCapability(_:)] to grant a media playback and capture capability, which you create with [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/ProcessCapability/mediaPlaybackAndCapture(environment:)]. Call [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/MediaEnvironment/createXPCRepresentation()] and use [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/XPC] to send the media environment to the rendering extension. Additionally, grant the same capability to the web content extension for the page that’s playing or capturing media, by calling [doc://com.apple.BrowserEngineKit/documentation/BrowserEngineKit/WebContentProcess/grantCapability(_:)].

## Creating a media environment

- **init(webPage:)**: Creates a new media environment identified by the URL.
- **init(xpcRepresentation:)**: Creates a media environment from an XPC representation.

## Sending media environments over XPC connections

- **createXPCRepresentation()**: Creates an encoded representation of the media environment, suitable for sending over an XPC connection.

## Capturing media streams

- **activate()**: Activates the media environment.
- **makeCaptureSession()**: Creates a new capture session in this media environment  or throws an error if it can not be created.
- **suspend()**: Suspends the media environment.

## Extension capabilities

- **ProcessCapability**: An enumeration that identifies capabilities that a browser app can grant to its extension processes.
